Introduction¶

Process mining helps uncover insights from data that represent different events in a process. It is valuable for understanding workflows, finding inefficiencies, and optimizing development processes. Using Kaiaulu for process mining enables this discovery for Github Events (e.g. creating issues, push events, deleting branches, etc). Allowing users to visualize and analyze event data effectively. This notebook demonstrates how to use Kaiaulu to download and parse Github Event data. As well how to interact with the data to leverage process discovery for deeper analysis. This video provides an overview of process mining concepts and its applications and can be used as a reference point to begin. This notebook heavily relies on the python package pm4py to create event mappings.

Requirements¶

  • Gihub Access Token
  • Kaiaulu R package to download data via CLI
  • Python environment with pm4py installed
In [98]:
# Python Imports 

import argparse
import pandas
import os
from datetime import datetime
import pm4py
import subprocess
import yaml

from pm4py.algo.discovery.dfg import algorithm as dfg_discovery
from pm4py.algo.discovery.heuristics import algorithm as heuristics_miner

Download and Parse data with Kaiaulu ghevents.R (CLI)¶

In [ ]:
# Save and set the current working directory to Kaiaulu
cwd = os.getcwd()
os.chdir(os.path.expanduser("~/Desktop/Kaiaulu/Working_issues/kaiaulu/"))

# To download use the download command specifing the <config_file> <github_token>

command = f"Rscript exec/ghevents.R download conf/kaiaulu.yml --token_path=~/.ssh/github_token"
subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True)
In [ ]:
# To Parse use the parse command and specify the <config_path> <output_dir>

command = f"Rscript exec/ghevents.R parse conf/kaiaulu.yml ../rawdata/issue_output.csv ../rawdata/commit_output.csv"
subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True)

Get Start and End Activies From Event Logs¶

In [ ]:
# Specify the csv file path <csv_path>
csv_path = "../rawdata/issue_output.csv"

def start_end_activities(csv_path): 
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    event_log = pm4py.format_dataframe(event_log, case_id='id', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')
    start_activities = pm4py.get_start_activities(event_log)
    end_activities = pm4py.get_end_activities(event_log)
    return start_activities, end_activities

print(start_end_activities(csv_path))

Generate Tree from Event Logs¶

In [102]:
# Specify a output_dir where the images will be sent, if it doesnt exist it will be created
output_dir = "../rawdata/images/"

def generate_tree(csv_path, output_dir):
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    event_log = pm4py.format_dataframe(event_log, case_id='issue_number', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')

    # Create if folder doesn't exist
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # Set up path and name of .png
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    file_name = f"process_tree_{timestamp}.png"
    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, file_name)

    process_tree = pm4py.discover_process_tree_inductive(event_log)
    pm4py.save_vis_process_tree(process_tree, output_path)
    print("Tree generated and saved")

generate_tree(csv_path, output_dir)

Tree generated and saved

Generate Graph from Event Logs¶

In [ ]:
# Pass the same arguments as trees

def generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir):
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    event_log = pm4py.format_dataframe(event_log, case_id='issue_number', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')
    
    # Create folder if it doesn't exist
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # Set up path and name of .png
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    file_name = f"process_graph_{timestamp}.png"
    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, file_name)

    process_tree = pm4py.discover_process_tree_inductive(event_log)
    bpmn_model = pm4py.convert_to_bpmn(process_tree)
    pm4py.save_vis_bpmn(bpmn_model, output_path, format="png")
    print("Graph generated and saved")

generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir)

Graph generated and saved

Generate Performance Graph: Time or Occurance Edge Weights¶

Time Edge Weight Graph¶

In [120]:
def generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir):
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    event_log['issue_number'] = event_log['issue_number'].astype(str)
    event_log['created_at'] = pandas.to_datetime(event_log['created_at'], errors='coerce')

    # Create folder if it doesn't exist
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
    
    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    file_name = f"performance_dfg_{timestamp}.png"
    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, file_name)
    
    performance_dfg, start_activities, end_activities = pm4py.discover_performance_dfg(event_log, case_id_key='issue_number', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')
    pm4py.save_vis_performance_dfg(performance_dfg, start_activities, end_activities, output_path)


generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir)

Generate Oc¶

In [122]:
def generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir):
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    event_log['issue_number'] = event_log['issue_number'].astype(str)
    event_log['created_at'] = pandas.to_datetime(event_log['created_at'], errors='coerce')

    # Create folder if it doesn't exist
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    file_name = f"performance_dfg_{timestamp}.png"
    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, file_name)
    
    performance_dfg, start_activities, end_activities = pm4py.discover_performance_dfg(event_log, case_id_key='issue_number', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')
    dfg, start_activities, end_activities = pm4py.discover_dfg(event_log, case_id_key='issue_number', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')
    pm4py.save_vis_dfg(dfg, start_activities, end_activities, output_path)


generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir)
In [117]:
# gerenate dotted chart

def generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir):
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    event_log['issue_number'] = event_log['issue_number'].astype(str)
    event_log['created_at'] = pandas.to_datetime(event_log['created_at'], errors='coerce')
    
    pm4py.save_vis_dotted_chart(event_log, '../rawdata/dotted.png', attributes=['created_at', 'event', 'actor_login'])


generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir)
In [118]:
def generate_graph_with_heuristics(csv_path, output_dir):
    # Read the CSV file into a pandas DataFrame
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    
    # Format the event log for PM4Py
    event_log = pm4py.format_dataframe(event_log, case_id='issue_number', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')

    # Create the output directory if it doesn't exist
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # Set up path and name of .png
    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    file_name = f"process_graph_{timestamp}.png"
    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, file_name)

    # Apply Heuristics Miner
    from pm4py.algo.discovery.heuristics import algorithm as heuristics_miner
    net, im, fm = heuristics_miner.apply(event_log)

    # Visualize the process model and save as PNG
    pm4py.view_petri_net(net, im, fm)
    pm4py.save_vis_petri_net(net, im, fm, output_path, format="png")

    print(f"Graph generated and saved using Heuristics Miner at {output_path}")

generate_graph_with_heuristics(csv_path, output_dir)

Graph generated and saved using Heuristics Miner at ../rawdata/images/process_graph_20250327_195813.png
In [119]:
# gerenate graph with filtering: 

# Pass the same arguments as trees

def generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir):
    event_log = pandas.read_csv(csv_path)
    event_log = pm4py.format_dataframe(event_log, case_id='issue_number', activity_key='event', timestamp_key='created_at')
    
    # Create if folder doesn't exist
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # Set up path and name of .png
    output_dir = os.path.expanduser(output_dir)
    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    file_name = f"process_graph_{timestamp}.png"
    output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, file_name)

    bpmn_filtered = pm4py.discover_bpmn_inductive(event_log, 0.8)
    pm4py.view_bpmn(bpmn_filtered)
    pm4py.save_vis_bpmn(bpmn_filtered, output_path, format="png")
    print("Graph generated and saved")

generate_graph(csv_path, output_dir)

Graph generated and saved
